title: Eschatology: The Great Tribulation (cont’d)
speaker: Pastor Tito Lyro
source_files: Lesson 10 on Eschatology_ The Great Tribulation (cont’d).txt
outline: Review of historic covenantal premillennialism timeline
Structure of the Olivet Discourse (Matthew 24, Mark 13, Luke 21)
Marks of the tribulation: false religion, civil unrest, worldwide evangelism, the lawless one
The days of Noah as pattern for the tribulation
Introduction to the rapture question and 1 Thessalonians 4:15-17
summary: This lesson continues the eschatology series with a focus on the Great Tribulation as taught in the Olivet Discourse (Matthew 24). After reviewing the timeline of historic covenantal premillennialism, the lesson examines the structure and content of Jesus's teaching about the tribulation, including marks such as false Christs and prophets, civil unrest among nations, worldwide gospel proclamation, and the revealing of the lawless one. Jesus's analogy of the days of Noah is explored as a pattern for the tribulation, showing that believers will be aware and prepared while unbelievers disregard all warnings. The lesson concludes with a preview of the rapture question to be addressed in the following lesson through 1 Thessalonians 4.
transcript:
## Introduction

As we wait for the equipment to turn on — would you in your Bible turn to Matthew chapter 24? That’s what we’re going to read from today. We’ll be reading verses 15 through 28, and then continuing on. We’re going to hopefully finish the subject of the tribulation as taught in the Scriptures today.

It’s impossible to talk about the tribulation without also talking about the coming of Christ, because in theology it is always put in terms of the return of Christ and the resurrection. You can see that we can divide these things into categories so that makes it a little easier for us to study them, but they really go together.

Matthew 24, starting at verse 15: *’Therefore when you see the ‘abomination of desolation,’ spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (whoever reads, let him understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains. Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take anything out of his house. And let him who is in the field not go back to get his clothes. But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days! And pray that your flight may not be in winter or on the Sabbath. For then there will be great tribulation, such as has not been since the beginning of the world until this time, no, nor ever shall be. And unless those days were shortened, no flesh would be saved; but for the elect’s sake those days will be shortened. Then if anyone says to you, \”Look, here is the Christ!\” or \”There!\” do not believe it. For false christs and false prophets will rise and show great signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect. See, I have told you beforehand. Therefore if they say to you, \”Look, He is in the desert!\” do not go out; or \”Look, He is in the inner rooms!\” do not believe it. For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes to the west, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be. For wherever the carcass is, there the eagles will be gathered together.*

*Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken. Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory. And He will send His angels with a great sound of a trumpet, and they will gather together His elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.’*

Although our Lord has given us a lot of information about the events surrounding His coming, He also told us to look for signs, to be aware of things going on around the time He comes.

## Review: Timeline of Historic Covenantal Premillennialism

Let me review the timeline we are working from. Great Tribulation — still future. Physical, bodily return of Christ and the resurrection of believers (Revelation 4-24). The new heaven and earth happen at that time. Now I want to ask you: when does the new heaven and earth happen in your scheme? In my scheme, they happen at the coming of Jesus Christ — which means that the curse of sin will be removed, but there will be sinful people on this earth during the millennium. I understand that is what I am saying.

There are two precedents for this in the Bible. One is Adam and Eve’s presence in the garden while sinners — before the curse was pronounced, they existed as sinful beings in a yet-uncursed world. The other is the other way around: Jesus with His resurrection body present in a sin-cursed world. So you have a precedent for this idea.

That is followed by a thousand-year physical reign on earth — the number one thousand is in Revelation 20. Followed by a rebellion of unbelievers led by Satan. Then the resurrection of unbelievers for judgment. Then the final judgment. Then the eternal state — which will look a lot like the millennium, except no sinners will be present.

## The Structure of the Olivet Discourse

Most important to understand is that Matthew 24, Mark 13, and Luke 21 are structured in response to a question asked by the disciples. In Matthew 24:3: *’Tell us, when will these things be? And what will be the sign of Your coming, and of the end of the age?’* That is a three-part question. Jesus is not trying to address everything concerning His return — He is answering the question the disciples asked, in the order they asked it.

As we read through this chapter, notice that it attaches the tribulation to the physical, bodily return of Christ. Verse 29: *’Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened... then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven... and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.’* So Jesus puts His return right after the events of the tribulation.

Now, the disciples as a group can be understood in two ways: as the specific men who were with Jesus, or as representative of disciples from all ages. We do the same kind of thing in Matthew 16 (keys to the kingdom), Matthew 28 (the Great Commission to make disciples of all nations). So we can understand the disciples here as representing all disciples who will be present at the coming of Jesus Christ.

Also, Bible prophecy often refers to events that happen at different times within a single prophecy — like Isaiah 7 (sign to Ahaz) and the birth of the Messiah appearing together, seen from a prophetic distance as one. I would suggest that the Olivet Discourse speaks both of the destruction of Jerusalem (especially clear in Luke 21) and of the ultimate Great Tribulation — with Jesus moving back and forth between these chronologically separated events. The destruction of Jerusalem is a type, a microcosm, a test case of what will happen at the return of Jesus Christ.

## Marks of the Tribulation

The tribulation will be marked by false religious claims. Verses 4-5: *’Take heed that no one deceives you. For many will come in My name, saying, \”I am the Christ,\” and will deceive many.’* Verse 11: *’Then many false prophets will rise up and deceive many.’* Verse 24: *’For false christs and false prophets will rise and show great signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect.’*

This agrees with Paul’s teaching in 2 Thessalonians 2:5-12: *’The coming of the lawless one is according to the working of Satan, with all power, signs, and lying wonders, and with all unrighteous deception among those who perish, because they did not receive the love of the truth that they might be saved. And for this reason God will send them strong delusion, that they should believe the lie, that they all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.’*

The tribulation will also include great civil unrest. Verses 7-8: *’For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. And there will be famines, pestilences, and earthquakes in various places. All these are the beginning of sorrows.’* And notice — not local unrest, but nations against nations and kingdoms against kingdoms.

Also: the end will not come until the entire world has been evangelized. Verse 14: *’And this gospel of the kingdom will be preached in all the world as a witness to all the nations, and then the end will come.’* Being evangelized is not the same as being saved. Jesus is not claiming all the world will be saved, but that all the world will hear the gospel — and that has not yet happened.

The tribulation will include a leading figure or system directing nations away from Christ. 2 Thessalonians 2 describes ‘the lawless one’ whom the Lord will consume with the breath of His mouth at His coming. John in 1 John calls this the anti-Christ. There is a figure or system that will lead nations away from Christ — Paul says it is currently being restrained, and when the restrainer is removed, the lawless one will be revealed.

## The Days of Noah

Jesus equates the tribulation with the days of Noah. Matthew 24:36-39:

> *\”But as the days of Noah were, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be. For as in the days before the flood, they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark, and did not know until the flood came and took them all away.\”* (Matthew 24:36-39)

The one event Jesus uses to illustrate this tribulation is not a localized event — it was universal, covering the whole world. Believers (Noah’s family) were aware of the tribulation and prepared. Unbelievers disregarded all warnings — Noah preached repentance for 120 years as he built the ark, and the people disregarded it entirely. They were immensely immoral and refused to hear what God was saying.

## What About the Rapture?

The word ‘rapture’ comes from a French word meaning ‘carrying off.’ In theology it usually refers to a secret return of Christ and secret removal of all believers from earth prior to the great tribulation. This doctrine is seen in texts like Matthew 24:40-41: *’Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left. Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.’* But the ultimate passage for the rapture, according to those who believe in a secret pre-tribulation removal, is 1 Thessalonians 4:15-17. And that is where we begin next Lord’s Day, Lord willing.

Let’s pray together. Father in heaven, thank you so much for your goodness to us. We thank you that you are a God who speaks to us. We thank you that we can be assured that our Lord Jesus is coming back for us, and that we will be with Him forever. We thank you that we can live in that hope even now and can worship you this morning in that hope. Bless us as we move into our time of worship. In Jesus’ name, amen.
